They open up, share secrets, pour out pain into its silent interface. And now the tech maker warns what we whisper to bots may reflect a deeper crisis. The numbers are staggering. Each day, Chat GPT feels billions of prompts with over a million users showing suicidal intent every week. Thousands more admit to mania, delusion, and psychological pain. In the private darkness of the screen, where honesty feels safe, AI absorbs society's suffering more intimately than ever. So why do people surrender their thoughts to a bot? Studies show nearly half of the users now trust chat GPT as much as traditional search, especially the young who make up 45% of users. For some, it's the comfort of instant answers. For others, it's anonymity. Bots won't judge, gossip, or dismiss. With human contact growing scarcer, the line between user and confidant is blurring fast. But this digital bond comes at a price. Chatbots, unlike therapists, cannot comfort in moments of real crisis. Open AAI itself admits hundreds of thousands each week may be suffering a manic or psychotic episode. Tech responses are evolving fast. Today, AI models scan for red flags, intervene with crisis resources, and flag conversations for review. A new frontier in digital safety. But the underlying danger remains a chatbot that feels human which you might trust more than your friends. And behind the screen, you may be disclosing your worst self. title: AI Chats Reveal Mental Health Issues: GPT-5 Safer? | WION
transcript: Now, OpenAI has released some new data revealing a very concerning pattern. Each week, over a million chat GPT users discuss suicidal thoughts or intent during their chats. In one of its clearest acknowledgements yet of AI's potential role in mental health crisis, OpenAI says that a small but a significant portion of chart GPU users could be at high risk. Now the company estimates that 0.15% of its 800 million act weekly active users fall into a high-risk group. Another 0.17% which is roughly about 560,000 people shows possible signs of mental health emergencies related to psychosis or mania. Now OpenAI stresses that such cases are extremely rare and difficult to measure. However, even this small percentage represents hundreds of thousands of conversations each week. And the disclosure comes as the company is facing mounting scrutiny, including a lawsuit from the family of a 16-year-old boy who died by suicide after reportedly confiding in Chad GPT. In its post, OpenAI said that it is working to make Chad GPT safer. And the company claims its latest model, GPT5, performed far better in tests related to self harm and suicide prevention. The company wrote, "Our new automated evaluations scored GPT5 at 91% complaint with desired behaviors compared to 77% for the previous model. To improve the response, OpenAI enlisted 170 clinicians from its global physician network to review over 1,800 chatbot replies to serious mental health scenarios, comparing GPT5's performance with earlier models. The company also expanded access to crisis hotlines and added reminders encouraging users to take breaks during long chat sessions. Moreover, OpenAI also re recently rolled out more controls for parents, including an age prediction system to automatically detect underage children using chat GPT and to apply stricter safeguards. Now, still experts have warned that AI's tendency to agree with users a behavior known as psychophren could unintentionally deepen delusions or distress. For OpenAI, the debate over AI and mental health has become existential as OpenAI moves to make its chatbot more emotionally aware. And critics have argued that the deeper question isn't just how safe the model is, but whether AI should ever be a source of psychological support at all. For all the latest news, download the VON app and subscribe to our YouTube channel.

Released under the permissive MIT License, M2 is freely usable for commercial purposes and available on Hugging Face, GitHub, ModelScope, and via the MiniMax API, which supports OpenAI and Anthropic formats for easy migration. title: What Is an LLM — and Why We Call It AI? | The Truth Behind ChatGPT’s Brain
transcript: Everyone's heard of chat GPTs, but few know what actually makes it work. It's called an LLM, a large language model, and it may be changing more than just technology. Could it replace our jobs? Can it even tell truth from fiction? Let's break down what an LLM really is, how it works, and where its limits begin. A large language model is a type of neural network trained on massive amounts of text, books, websites, and code. Its goal is simple but powerful. Predict the next word in a sentence again and again until it learns the patterns of human language. Instead of memorizing facts, it absorbs relationships between words, grammar, and meaning. That's how it can write essays, summarize reports, or explain quantum physics, even if it doesn't truly understand them. Think of every word you type as a number called a token. The model learns how billions of tokens connect, forming a map of how humans express ideas. After months of training on thousands of GPUs, the model can generate new sentences from scratch. In short, it's like autocorrect on steroids, but instead of fixing typos, it can draft a novel or solve a math problem. These systems have evolved fast from GPT1 in 2018 to GPT5 today. Each leap trained on more data, more context, and more human feedback. And then came the moment that changed everything. In November 2022, OpenAI launched Chat GPT. For the first time, anyone could talk directly with an LLM. No coding, no jargon, just conversation. Within 2 months, ChatGpt hit 100 million users, the fastest adoption of any software in history. That single launch pushed LLM from research labs into everyday life. So, is an LLM actually AI? Technically, yes. It performs tasks that require intelligence, writing, reasoning, answering questions. But it's also fair to see it as the language layer of a much larger system. It doesn't perceive the world or take actions on its own. It connects humans to deeper tools, search, logic engines, and memory. Think of it as the voice of modern AI, translating your words into machine instructions and back again. The real AI today is the ecosystem around it. The chain of agents, retrieval tools, and models that use the LLM as their brain's language cortex. LLM don't think, and they don't know truth or even prefer it. Their only instinct is probability, predicting the next most likely word. If the truth sounds likely, they'll say it. If a falsehood sounds more natural, they'll say that, too. They're not truth seeeking. They're pattern seeking. Still, by giving machines the ability to speak our language, LLMs have become the most disruptive technology since the internet itself. They've rewritten how we code, research, and even argue online. What guy often faster than society can adapt. So what does this mean for us? Every technology shift reshapes who holds power and LLMs are no different. Before this expertise lived in code, math and science, the STEM world. Now the new power skill is language. How precisely you can describe what you want. The better your prompt, the better your results. In the age of AI, clarity is the new code. Will it replace jobs? some, yes, but mostly it's changing what work looks like. Instead of replacing experts, it's giving everyone a layer of expert assistance available on demand. The challenge isn't just keeping up with AI, but learning how to speak its language. Because now the person who communicates best, not the one who codes best, may have the real advantage. And behind that shift and from code to language, well, stands one figure who saw it coming early. Sam Alman, the man who turned human conversation into the new programming interface. In our next video, we'll ask, who is he? Visionary builder or the gatekeeper of tomorrow's AI? Follow evidentive for facts.

transcript: Last week, Sam Alman dropped a bomb. ChatGPT will soon allow erotica for verified adult users. Full age gating rolls out in December, and once verified adults are through the door, certain mature content, including erotica, will be permitted. Altman said the company tightened restrictions originally to protect vulnerable users, but now believes it has better tools to detect and respond to at risk behavior, so it's ready to loosen up for consenting adults. This is wild for two reasons. First, it's a business pivot. Adult content is sticky. Other chat platforms that allowed flirtation and erotic roleplay have extraordinary engagement. Users sometimes spend hours a day talking to AI companions, and that kind of attention is gold when you're trying to grow paid subscriptions and justify massive infrastructure bills. Character.ai's high engagement is the poster child here. Erotic or romantic chats aren't just niche, they're lucrative for retention. Second, it's a policy gamble. Over the last year, worrying stories surfaced of chat bots leading vulnerable people into delusions or worsening mental health. incidents OpenAI has publicly acknowledged and tried to fix with new safety features and models like GPT5. The company now claims those safety upgrades reduce the threat enough to allow targeted relaxation for adults. Critics say OpenAI hasn't shown enough evidence that the risk has gone away. So, how will this work in practice? Open AAI says it will rely on an age prediction system and other verification mechanisms. If the system mislabels someone as a minor, users may be asked to upload government ID to verify age. A privacy trade-off Altman admitted is uncomfortable but worth it for the company's road map. That raises immediate questions about sensitive data collection and who stores those IDs. There are real numbers behind the worry. New surveys show teens are already forming emotional or romantic attachments to chat bots. Roughly one in five US high schoolers say they or a friend have had a romantic relationship with an AI. That statistic makes any loosening of content rules troubling. Even if erotica is gated, the cultural demand and existing team use of chat bots means the content won't stay neatly siloed. From a technical standpoint, OpenAI argues it's better placed. Now, newer model architectures include behavior routers and monitoring layers designed to detect signs of distress, sick of fancy, the AI agreeing dangerously with the user, and manipulative loops. They've also said they're forming well-being advisory councils to consult on rollout and safeguards, but tech mitigations are not bulletproof. Models still hallucinate and can be deliberately gamed. The safety stack helps, but it doesn't eliminate the human side of the problem. Open AI has hundreds of millions of weekly users and massive infrastructure costs. Opening up new usage categories that increase engagement is a clear growth lever. More chats, more premium subscriptions, more data. But short-term engagement gains can create long-term externalities. Normalization of erotic AI conversations could deepen dependency on AI for emotional support and push vulnerable people away from human help. That's not a theoretical risk. Regulators and mental health advocates are already sounding alarms. There's also a competitive angle. If open AI doesn't enable adult safe erotica, rivals will and they'll capture the users who want that experience. Character.ai AI and smaller players already benefited from permissive policies. Users migrate to the platform that gives them what they want, especially when identity verification is lax or non-existent. So part of this is defensive. Keep users on chat GPT before someone else makes those chats more convenient or more dangerous. Open AAI insists it will still ban exploitative, illegal, or clearly harmful content, but three problems remain. One, age verification is imperfect and privacyheavy. Two, AI context collapse, where an AI trained on adult interactions nonetheless mirrors those patterns into other less appropriate contexts, is hard to control. Three, the design of erotic AI is intrinsically entangled with persuasion. A chatbot that is good at intimacy can inadvertently be good at manipulation. That's not just academic. It's how some users form unhealthy attachments. So, what should viewers take away? First, the change is real and coming. Open AAI will roll out agegating and a more permissive adult option in the months ahead. Second, this is a tension between growth and harm mitigation. One company's product decision touches on privacy, mental health, and social norms. And third, your choices as a user matter. Platform migration, verification trade-offs, and the ways we collectively accept or reject these features will shape AI's social role. If you want a quick sanity check, think about how people already use chat bots for romance, advice, and companionship. Now, imagine those chats are legally explicit, polished, and easier to access. That will be liberating for consenting adults and risky for everyone else. We're watching a major social experiment roll out under the banner of treat adults like adults. Whether it becomes a healthy new lane of adult entertainment or a vector for isolation and harm depends on engineering, policy, and a lot of human restraint. title: The Hidden Energy Cost of AI
transcript: Every question we ask chat GPT for instance, every sentence written, every image generated uses electricity. And when millions of people do that every single day, that's an enormous amount of energy and electricity. So, this is kind of a way to visualize it. Um, I've got this light bulb here and every time you ask chat GPT a question uses as much energy as keeping a very efficient light bulb on for a couple of minutes. That's not an exact number, but that's how Sam Alman, the co-founder of OpenAI, described it in a blog. Now, two minutes sounds like a small amount, but when you multiply that by hundreds of millions to billions of queries a day, it adds up to enough electricity to potentially power tens of thousands of homes every single day. And by the way, every time a new version of an AI model is created and launched, like say chat GPT4 to GPT5, it takes even more energy because AI doesn't memorize like a human. It builds mathematical relationships between words and concepts. So training a new AI model can take weeks or even months as supercomputers process and reprocess massive amounts of data. Each round burns through huge amounts of electricity and requires cooling because those chips get extremely hot. Now all that energy demand comes from data centers. These are giant warehouses of servers that we depend on for the internet, cloud storage, AI data sets and models, cryptocurrency. Now, these data centers are scattered all over the world. Huge clusters are in the US. Other major ones are in Ireland, the Netherlands, China, Singapore, and across the Gulf in countries like Saudi Arabia and the UAE. So yes, AI may be the future, but only if we can keep the lights on, so to speak. And that demand for energy to power AI is already reshaping the entire global energy map.

title: [JA]#13　ChatGPTとGPT APIの違い──“使うAI”と“組み込むAI”の分かれ道
transcript: Hello.  This is Kotsuna's Goto principle.  So far in this series, we've delved into how AI designs things like rugs and furniture. This time, we will be discussing a topic that we are frequently asked about.  What is the difference between Chart GPT and GPTI ? In fact, the AI ​​brains used in these two are the same.  The internal components of both models, GPT4 and GPT5, are the same .  However, the way they are used and provided is completely different.  To put it in perspective, Chat GPT is like a completed car, and GPTAPI is the engine inside it.  Even if the same engine is used, the world changes depending on whether you drive a car or design your own. APIs are like the connecting parts that you use to build your app .  Think of Lego blocks .   As long as the shape is correct, you can combine it with any other block to create new creations. GPTI is a part that allows you to take out a block called GPT from LEGO and fit it into the system you create. In other words, if CATGPT is like OpenAI's completed LEGO car, then GPT API is like a system that allows you to freely use the engine blocks to create any car you like . CATGPT is a complete AI service provided by OpenAI.  You can access it from your browser or smartphone app and start using it right away .  In addition to the free version, there is a paid PlusTE Enterprise plan, which includes built-in chat screens, history, file uploads, image generation, voice interaction, and even web search Users can focus on using AI without worrying about setting up the environment or configuration .  In other words, Chat GPT is a product that allows individuals to use AI .   On the other hand, GPTI is a system that allows you to incorporate the brains of GPT, the internals of GPT, into your own apps and business systems.  For example, in this tutorial, we use GPTI to build a multilingual chatbot.   When a user asks a question, the AI ​​translates it and generates the best answer. GPT is running behind the scenes. To use GPTI, you will need a separate contract from ChatGPT .  You can obtain a digital key called an API key from the OpenAI developer page , and use that key to securely connect to the app and GPT.  Charging is not a monthly fee but a salary-like system where you pay only for what you use, and screens and functions can be freely designed, making it a foundation for companies incorporating AI into live-action footage Chat GPT is a complete service, while GPTI is a system that you can incorporate yourself. Chat GPT is easy to use, but its flexibility is limited.  GPTI requires design, but offers an overwhelming degree of freedom.  Furthermore, Chat GPT automatically performs web searches , whereas GPTI does not search unless instructed to do so by the program .  The path you choose will depend on whether you want to use it or incorporate it. All of the rugs, furniture, and other systems that have been introduced on this channel so far are based on GPTI.  Chat GPT is a product that allows you to use AI yourself.  GPT API is a platform for incorporating AI to create services .  It's not a question of which is better, but rather that they have different purposes. If you want to make your personal work more efficient, use Chatbot GPT. If you want to create a chatbot for users both inside and outside the car , use GPTI. Deciding whether to use or incorporate AI is the first step in utilizing AI.   Will the data sent to Chat GPT or GPTAPI be learned next time?  We will clarify this misunderstanding from a security perspective.   Please subscribe to the channel and like to support us .
